title: Israeli strike kills three Gaza Palestinians, including a child, ministry says
gist: Despite a US-mediated Gaza ceasefire nominally in effect since October, Israeli strikes continue: three Palestinians including a child were killed in Deir al-Balah, with 1,045 Palestinians and four Israeli soldiers killed in the eight months since the truce.

insights.claim: The Gaza ceasefire nominally in effect since October 2025 has not stopped Israeli strikes, with 1,045 Palestinians and four Israeli soldiers killed in the eight months since it took effect.
insights.speaker: Mahmoud Issa
insights.so_what: A 'ceasefire' with ongoing four-figure casualties signals the Gaza file remains an active conflict risk, not a resolved one — relevant to regional escalation tail risk.
insights.quote: Since the ceasefire took effect eight months ago, 1,045 Palestinians and four Israeli soldiers have been killed in Gaza, according to figures from each side.
insights.locator: Since the ceasefire took effect eight months ago

insights.claim: Israel continues to occupy roughly 60% of Gaza territory even under the standing ceasefire.
insights.speaker: Mahmoud Issa
insights.so_what: Large standing occupation footprint means the territorial status quo is unsettled, keeping a structural flashpoint open.
insights.locator: which is under the control of Hamas

insights.claim: The US-appointed Board of Peace envoy to Gaza has stated both Israel and Hamas have violated the ceasefire agreement, undercutting either side's claim to compliance.
insights.speaker: Mahmoud Issa
insights.so_what: Mutual-violation framing by the US envoy lowers the credibility of the truce holding, raising the probability of renewed open conflict.
insights.quote: Nikolay Mladenov, the U.S. President Donald Trump-appointed Board of Peace envoy to Gaza, has said both parties have violated the agreement.
insights.locator: Hamas has accused Israel of violating the ceasefire
